title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for VPC  Network Route Changes

input_row.title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for VPC  Network Route Changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) network route changes.
input_row.rationale: Google Cloud Platform (GCP) routes define the paths network traffic takes from a VM instance to another destination. The other destination can be inside the organization VPC network (such as another VM) or outside of it. Every route consists of a destination and a next hop. Traffic whose destination IP is within the destination range is sent to the next hop for delivery. Monitoring changes to route tables will help ensure that all VPC traffic flows through an expected path.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console Ensure that the prescribed Log metric is present: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. In the User-defined Metrics section, ensure that at least one metric is present with the filter text: resource.type="gce_route" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.insert") Ensure the prescribed alerting policy is present: 3. Go to Alerting by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerting. 4. Under the Policies section, ensure that at least one alert policy exists for the log metric above. Clicking on the policy should show that it is configured with a condition. For example, Violates when: Any logging.googleapis.com/user/ stream is above a threshold of 0 for greater than zero(0) seconds means that the alert will trigger for any new owner change. Verify that the chosen alert thresholds make sense for the user's organization. 5. Ensure that the appropriate notification channels have been set up. From Google Cloud CLI Ensure the prescribed log metric is present: 1. List the log metrics: gcloud logging metrics list --format json 2. Ensure that the output contains at least one metric with the filter set to: resource.type="gce_route" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.insert") 3. Note the value of the property metricDescriptor.type for the identified metric, in the format logging.googleapis.com/user/. Ensure that the prescribed alerting policy is present: 4. List the alerting policies: gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json 5. Ensure that the output contains an least one alert policy where: • conditions.conditionThreshold.filter is set to metric.type=\"logging.googleapis.com/user/\" • AND enabled is set to true
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console Create the prescribed Log Metric: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics and click "CREATE METRIC". 2. Click the down arrow symbol on the Filter Bar at the rightmost corner and select Convert to Advanced Filter 3. Clear any text and add: resource.type="gce_route" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.insert") 4. Click Submit Filter. Display logs appear based on the filter text entered by the user. 5. In the Metric Editor menu on the right, fill out the name field. Set Units to 1 (default) and Type to Counter. This ensures that the log metric counts the number of log entries matching the user's advanced logs query. 6. Click Create Metric. Create the prescribed alert policy: 1. Identify the newly created metric under the section User-defined Metrics at https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. Click the 3-dot icon in the rightmost column for the new metric and select Create alert from Metric. A new page displays. 3. Fill out the alert policy configuration and click Save. Choose the alerting threshold and configuration that makes sense for the user's organization. For example, a threshold of zero(0) for the most recent value ensures that a notification is triggered for every owner change in the project: Set `Aggregator` to `Count` Set `Configuration`: - Condition: above - Threshold: 0 - For: most recent value 4. Configure the desired notification channels in the section Notifications. 5. Name the policy and click Save. From Google Cloud CLI Create the prescribed Log Metric: • Use the command: gcloud logging metrics create Create the prescribed the alert policy: • Use the command: gcloud alpha monitoring policies create
input_row.impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage. These charges could be significant depending on the size of the organization.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit control specifies queries that can be automated via GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK commands.
- The requirements include checking for the existence of log metrics and alert policies, which can be performed programmatically.
- Specific commands using `gcloud` have been provided for listing metrics and policies, validating that this can be done without manual intervention.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_vpc_route_changes_log_metric_and_alerting_policy

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **Verify Log Metrics:**
   - Use the command: `gcloud logging metrics list --format json`
   - The script will check if there is at least one metric with the filter set to:
     ```
     resource.type="gce_route" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.insert")
     ```
   - **Pass Criteria**: At least one log metric matching the criteria exists.

2. **Verify Alerting Policy:**
   - Use the command: `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json`
   - The script will check for the presence of at least one alert policy where:
     - `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` is set to:
       ```
       metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/"
       ```
     - `enabled` is set to `true`.
   - **Pass Criteria**: At least one alert policy matching the criteria is active.

3. **Expected Output:**
   - The configuration should return data reflecting the presence of both the log metric and alerting policy with the necessary conditions.

By following this approach, we ensure comprehensive compliance verification while utilizing programmatic methods to affirm settings without manual checks.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The tasks involved, such as checking for the existence of log metrics and alert policies, can indeed be automated using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands. The use of `gcloud logging metrics list` and `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` supports this automation capability.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The use of `gcloud logging metrics list` to verify log metrics is appropriate. However, the `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` command is in alpha, which may not be suitable for production environments due to potential instability or changes. It would be better to use stable commands or APIs if available.
- The program name `gcp_logging_vpc_route_changes_log_metric_and_alerting_policy` is descriptive and follows a reasonable naming convention, but it could be more concise.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Command Stability:** Consider using stable versions of the `gcloud` commands. If `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` is necessary, ensure that the environment is prepared for potential changes in alpha features.
- **Program Name:** A more concise name could be `gcp_logging_vpc_route_changes_monitoring`. This name is still descriptive but shorter and focuses on the core task of monitoring.
- **Additional Checks:** Ensure that the alert policy specifically targets the log metric created for VPC route changes. This can be done by checking the `filter` field in the alert policy to ensure it matches the log metric's filter.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-founded in the capabilities of GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK. The approach is technically sound, with minor improvements suggested for command stability and naming conventions.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- Modify: Update the program name to `gcp_logging_vpc_route_changes_monitoring` for clarity and conciseness. Ensure the use of stable `gcloud` commands where possible, especially for production environments. Confirm that alert policies are specifically tied to the relevant log metrics for VPC route changes.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the tasks involved, such as checking for the existence of log metrics and alert policies, can be automated using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK commands. The necessary commands (`gcloud logging metrics list` and `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list`) are available to programmatically verify the presence of the required log metrics and alert policies. Although the use of an alpha command was noted, it is still feasible for automation with appropriate caution.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_vpc_route_changes_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:** Logging and Monitoring
- **API/CLI Method:** 
  - `gcloud logging metrics list --format json`
  - `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json`
- **Properties to Check:**
  - For log metrics: Filter text should be `resource.type="gce_route" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.routes.insert")`
  - For alert policies: `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` should include the log metric and `enabled` should be `true`.
- **Pass Criteria:** 
  - At least one log metric exists with the specified filter.
  - At least one alert policy is active and targets the log metric.
- **Fail Criteria:** 
  - No log metric with the specified filter exists.
  - No active alert policy targets the log metric.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions. Both agreed on the feasibility of automation, with the second opinion suggesting improvements for command stability and program name conciseness, which have been incorporated.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Google_Cloud_Platform_Foundation_Benchmark_v4_0_0
